threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'the Tube' instead of 'the Overground' like it's not a complete lifestyle change. That's an instant giveaway.
Caught a night bus from Brixton to Clapham and overheard a guy talking about his cat named 'Mr. Snuggles' – he said he’s preparing for his first birthday party. Brixton’s full of surprises!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I found a fresh corner store selling samosas at 2 AM, I realized this place never sleeps. That's the Neukölln spirit, isn't it?

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, you can find a café where they serve coffee with a side of political pamphlets. It’s like sipping activism with your espresso.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone's from here if they smell the BART equipment problem from a block away and still decide to take it anyway. That's true grit.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy in a tuxedo singing to tourists at the stop near Union Square. Muni’s never boring; you never know when it turns into a live show.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but the real charm lies in the little corner bars where locals argue about Barça over cheap vermut.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell them by their enthusiasm for making eye contact and asking for directions, even when they’re already on the right path.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a 'corner shop' instead of a 'local'. Only true Dalstonites know it’s all about the 'vintage thrift' vibe.
Caught a night bus home and the driver switched off the main lights, turning the ride into a disco with just the glowing exit signs. Unforgettable!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a goat munching on someone's garden while a couple argued in perfect harmony over the best döner nearby.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a döner place that stays open till 5 AM, serving up perfect late-night cabbage and mystery sauces while the night owls debate life.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: People from here know to check BART's status before heading to a show. Out-of-towners? They're still figuring out how to navigate those delays on a Friday.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus with a DJ set up in the back, blasting 90s hip-hop. Riders were bobbing their heads like it was a rooftop party.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists act like Gràcia's plazas are all about sunlight and tapas, but locals know they're often just a backdrop for street art and impromptu jam sessions.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by their bewildered expressions at the Mercat de l'Abaceria. They’re the ones still holding onto the map while trying to order a 'café con leche.'
